mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of instances when UNRATE spikes above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue visibility and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 52%).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods generate positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 5.0 percent imposes severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, compressing earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income, which directly fuels corporate sales growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC consistently below 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP and real personal spending confirms broad-based economic momentum, reducing default risk and triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The combined positive momentum of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to yield positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a financial stress regime indicates collapsing factory utilization and inventory drawdowns, which directly reduces corporate profit forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during financial stress periods produce positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases.
